sound_type: sustained howl -- deep, resonant, landscape-scale. 150-780 Hz fundamental. 3-11 seconds. 5-10 km carry. E=7
ecosystem: Wilderness. Remote mountain forests, alpine ridges, deep river valleys. Landscape-scale territory 200-1,000+ km2.
season: Year-round. Howling peaks in winter (territory) and spring (pup-rearing).
family: Canidae (third entry -- coyote howl 208, coyote pup 209)

coordinates.description: Gray Wolf in northeastern Washington Cascades. Recolonizing from BC/Idaho.

taxonomy_note.description: EXTIRPATED. Eliminated from Washington by 1930s. Returning from BC/Idaho. 30+ packs. Third Canidae. Canidae quartet apex.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Canidae: Coyote Howl (208, E=5), Coyote Pup Yipping (209, E=4), Gray Wolf Howl (210, E=7).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Canis lupus = 'wolf dog'. lupus from Latin for wolf.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Canis -- the dog genus. The wolf is the largest wild member.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Third Canidae entry. Apex predator.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Canidae: third entry. Quartet apex. Most powerful voice.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Largest canid in North America. 30-80 kg. Pack social structure.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Howl: 150-780 Hz. Bark: 200-900 Hz. Growl: 80-300 Hz. Whine: 300-1,500 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Howl: 3-11 sec per howl. Sessions: 30 sec to several minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single sustained howl. Rising then fading. Individual acoustic signature.
song_metrics.note_types: Howl (sustained, territory/contact), bark (short, alarm), growl (low, dominance), whine (high, submission).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Solo: repeated howls with pauses. Pack chorus: overlapping at different pitches.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, resonant, chest-amplified. Individual signature in pitch/duration/harmonics.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=7. Landscape-scale. 5-10 km carry. The most powerful voice in the Canidae family.
song_metrics.tempo: Howls every 10-30 seconds during session. Pack chorus overlapping.
song_metrics.context: Territory advertisement. Pack rally. Social bonding. Mourning. Year-round.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Individual variation strong. Regional dialects suggested but debated.
song_metrics.key_property: GRAY WOLF HOWL. Deep sustained. 150-780 Hz. E=7. EXTIRPATED/RETURNING. Third Canidae. Canidae quartet apex. Landscape-scale. Trophic cascade. v1.49.351.

pelage.overall: Large canid. 30-80 kg. Coat color varies: gray, brown, black, white. Thick undercoat. Bushy tail. Large paws.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Larger than coyote. Broader head. Shorter ears relative to head. Longer legs. Tail carried lower. Often gray with lighter underparts.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Thick winter coat, thinner summer coat. Seasonal molting.

physical_description.overall: 30-80 kg (65-175 lbs). Body length 1.0-1.5 m. Shoulder height 66-81 cm. Largest wild canid in North America.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Much larger than coyote. Broader muzzle. Proportionally shorter ears. Longer legs. Tail usually below horizontal. Pack social structure.
physical_description.field_id: Large canid in remote mountain terrain. Gray/brown/black coat. Broader head than coyote. Travels in family groups. If you hear a deep, sustained howl carrying across a valley -- wolf.

nodes.title: Apex Voice -- E=7 Canidae
nodes.description: Most powerful voice in the Canidae family. 5-10 km carry. Landscape-scale communication. The quartet's peak.

nodes.title: Extirpation and Return
nodes.description: Eliminated from Washington by 1930s. 70 years of silence. Returning from BC/Idaho. 30+ packs. Every pack a negotiation between wildness and agriculture.

nodes.title: Trophic Cascade -- Wolf Changes the River
nodes.description: Wolf changes elk behavior changes vegetation changes streams changes fish changes birds. The wolf reshapes the ecosystem by existing.

nodes.title: Pack Chorus -- Deliberate Dissonance
nodes.description: Wolves avoid matching pitch when howling together. Each wolf at different frequency. Acoustic illusion of larger pack. Intentional.

cross_references.relationship: CANIDAE COMPARISON! Coyote (E=5, urban-adapted) vs. Wolf (E=7, wilderness-dependent). Wolf outpowers coyote by 2 energy steps.
CANIDAE QUARTET! Pup yip (E=4) to wolf howl (E=7). The +3 energy leap. The quartet's dramatic spike.
CANIDAE QUARTET! Wolf howl (E=7) to fox bark (E=4). The -3 energy drop. Apex to compact.
LONE VOICE FILLS THE SPACE! Songwriter fills recording with layers. Wolf fills valley with one howl. Solo voices, maximum reach.
